dialogues.text_en: That sea-sloth Sevrin and his lot -- never around when there's work to be done!
I'd sooner hire you for the sake of gettin' things done, but then the rapscallions would never learn.
Round them up and let them know that Staelwyrn would like a word... And don't take “no” for an answer!
So, Staelwyrn sent you. And what do you reckon I say to that?
Damn right! Didn't get where I am by takin' guff from any glorified gardeners! ...But I s'pose I could see what he wants to get him off my back.
Axes were made for splittin' skulls, not stumps! You know what I did in my sailin' days?
You bet your beard / bosom I did, lad / lass ! Aye, those were the days... <sigh> I reckon now I'll have to be satisfied takin' my axe to the trees.
Aye, I'm Sevrin. You the old man's errand girl, now?
Tell my dear old captain not to wet his britches. I'll be along.
Oh, and just so we're clear -- I don't care for landlubbers. Go find the chocobo you rode in on, and ride on out.
<groan> How am I supposed to work on an empty stomach? Go on, have a guess at what I had for breakfast.

Wha-- Gods, how'd you know!? It's my breath, ain't it? It probably smells as wooden as the food tasted... But I know why you're here. <sigh> I'd just as soon get some real food in me, but I ain't one to reject a summons from the cap'n.
Work? Ha! Can't work when me hands are full liftin' this mug. Ye know, I'd wager me last gil ye'd never guess what I'm drinkin'.
Well, yer right about the “swill” part. I reckon it might be worth a day o' doin' Staelwyrn's chores if it'll afford me a taste o' proper grog.
You continue to impress, lad / lass . All but Sevrin are accounted for.
It's no fault of yours he squirmed out. I've let that worm wriggle through my fingers more times than I can count.
But rest assured, the next time I see him, I'll put him squarely in the dirt, where all such worms belong.
